description: Aptitude Inclination Indicator (Aii). 8 scenarios covering 6 aptitude dimensions. v2.3: prompts and vertex labels shortened for cognitive load reduction. Prompt carries full scenario; each vertex is one short idea. Measured dimensions, dimensionIds, framing, and coverage map unchanged.
scoring_note: score = (barycentric_coordinate x 4) + 1, mapping [0,1] to [1,5]. Aggregate by simple average per dimension.

triangles.title: Something Keeps Going Wrong
triangles.prompt: Your group has been doing something the same way for a while and it keeps breaking down. Nobody agrees on why. You're asked to figure it out. What do you do first?

a.label: Trace each step until the logic breaks

b.label: Look at the tools - something's set up wrong

c.label: Compare all the failures and find the pattern

triangles.tension_note: Signal: decompose logic (G), investigate system internals (T), or find structural similarity across failures (P).

triangles.title: The Unclear Instructions
triangles.prompt: Someone gives you instructions but you genuinely don't know what they want. You need to reply before you see them tomorrow. What do you do?

a.label: Find the exact words causing confusion, ask precisely

b.label: Guess what they mean, offer two or three directions

c.label: Work out what decisions must be made, ask only those

triangles.tension_note: Best V vs C separator. Verbal targets language itself; Creative generates options; Logical maps decision dependencies.

triangles.title: The New App
triangles.prompt: You get access to a new app or tool packed with features you've never used. How do you figure it out?

a.label: Dig into settings - understand how it's built first

b.label: Read the guide - know what it's for before using it

c.label: Just start - learn by actually doing something

triangles.tension_note: Technical: understand internals first. Verbal: extract meaning from docs. Creative: output over comprehension.

triangles.title: The Disagreement
triangles.prompt: Two people you know are arguing about how to do something. Both sound reasonable. They ask for your take. What do you do?

a.label: Ask what each person is actually assuming

b.label: Check if they're using the same words differently

c.label: Find data that could settle or inform it

triangles.tension_note: Strongest G vs V separator. Logical targets assumptions; Verbal targets word meaning; Quantitative seeks data.

triangles.title: Not What You Expected
triangles.prompt: You followed a process carefully - a recipe, an experiment, a plan - and got a result you didn't predict. Not a disaster, just unexpected. What do you do first?

a.label: Change one thing and try again

b.label: Think if you've seen this kind of result before

c.label: Retrace every step until you find where it went wrong

triangles.tension_note: Only triangle where C, P, and T appear together. None of the options is primed by the prompt.

triangles.title: The Big Decision
triangles.prompt: You have to decide something that really matters - which school, which path, which opportunity. You have some information but not enough. How do you think it through?

a.label: List the factors and put rough numbers on each option

b.label: Work out what each option would need to be true

c.label: Picture each option as a real future

triangles.tension_note: Only triangle pairing N directly against C. Personal framing relatable to teenagers.

triangles.title: The Group Project That Went Wrong
triangles.prompt: A group project fell apart because two people had totally different ideas of what they were making. You're trying to work out what went wrong. Where do you start?

a.label: Find the one phrase each person read differently

b.label: Ask each person what they thought it would look like

c.label: Map what each person did and find where they disconnected

triangles.tension_note: Verbal looks at language; Pattern surfaces misaligned mental models; Technical maps the process handoff.

triangles.title: Completely New Territory
triangles.prompt: You're getting into something you know nothing about - a new subject, skill, hobby, or field. What's your natural way in?

a.label: Find what it reminds you of and build from there

b.label: Find out what gets measured here - what counts as good

c.label: Understand how the tools and processes actually work

triangles.tension_note: Only triangle pairing N and T directly. Broad framing covers any new domain.
